title: Manage tombstones with the right compaction strategy
description: Match compaction strategy to the workload and avoid delete-heavy patterns that accumulate tombstones.
body: Use when a table sees deletes, TTLs, or frequent overwrites. Do not model a queue or a frequently-deleted dataset on a single partition with the default compaction; deletes become tombstones that linger until gc_grace_seconds passes and compaction runs, and reads must scan them. Actions: choose TimeWindowCompactionStrategy for time-series with TTL so whole SSTables expire together, avoid queue-like delete patterns, and monitor tombstones-per-read in tracing. Verify reads scan few tombstones. Validation signals include tracing showing a low tombstone scan count and TTL'd SSTables dropping whole. Risk boundary: a partition that crosses the tombstone failure threshold makes reads fail outright, so redesign delete-heavy access patterns rather than raising the threshold.
